content.full_text:
Fern Por-rrrcAl PnncrrcEs CoMMrssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 r Sacramenro. CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-,s660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
May 18,2009
Ernestine Jensen, Office Support Specialist
San Diego County Registrar of Voters
Campaign Services Section
5201 Ruffin Road, Suite I
Santa Diego, CA 92123-6665
Re:
Your Request for lnformal Assistance
Our File No.I-09-112
Dear Ms. Jensen:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign statement filing
officer duty provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Because your question seeks
general guidance and does not reference any specific proceedings, we are treating your request as
one for informal assistance.2
QUESTION
May cash be accepted as payment for a filing fee and./or candidate statement fee?
CONCLUSION
Yes, cash may be accepted from the candidate's personal funds for payment of his or her
filing fee and /or candidate statement fee. Cash may not be accepted from any other person on
behalf of the candidate for payment of the filing fee and"/or candidate statement fee.
' The Political Retbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8 I fi)O through 9 I 0 I 4. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections I 8 I l0 through I 8997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of the Calitirrnia Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.
: Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or ftrrmal
written advice. (Section 831 l4l Regulation 18329(c) (3).)


File No. l-09-II2
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are seeking advice on behalf of the San Diego County Registrar of Voters in your
capacity as an office support specialist in the Campaign Services Section. During the candidate
filing period, you collect filing fees and/or candidate statement fees. You also assist with
preparing a Candidate Filing Guide to assist candidates prior to every election. You are
inquiring whether cash can be accepted as payment for a filing fee and/or candidate statement fee
so that this information can be included in your Candidate Filing Guide.
ANALYSIS
Duties of the Filing Officer
Section 82027 defines a filing officer as the person or agency which receives and retains
original campaign statements. [n your agency's role as a filing officer for San Diego County,
your office is required to perform a number of duties with regard to campaign statements,
including supplying necessary forms and manuals to candidates. (Section 81010.) You are
requesting information as to whether it is proper to accept cash payments from candidates in
certain circumstances. This information will be included in the Candidate Filing Guide that is
being prepared for distribution.
Duty to File
The Act requires candidates to disclose receipt of contributions and expenditures made in
election campaigns in order that voters may be fully informed. (Section 81002.) The definition
of expenditure does not include a candidate's use of his or her own money to pay for either a
filing fee for a declaration of candidacy or a candidate statement prepared pursuant to Section
13307 of the Elections Code. (Section 82025.) In addition, payments from the candidate's
personal funds for a candidate filing fee or a candidate statement of qualifications fee are not
considered contributions or loans. (Section 85200.)
Section 84300(b) states that no expenditure of one hundred dollars ($tooy or more shall
be made in cash. However, since the payment of a filing fee or candidate statement fee is not
considered an expenditure, we conclude that your office may accept cash payments from a
candidate's personal funds for these fees. Please note that this advice is limited to payments
from the candidate's personal funds and does not apply to payments made on behalf of the
candidate by another petson3. Such a payment *ortAtonriitlte a political expenditure under
Section 84300(b) and cannot be paid in cash.
r "Person" means an individual, proprietorship, firm, partnership, joint venture, syndicate, business trust,
company. corporation, limited liability company, association, committee, and any other organization or group of
persons acting in concert. (Section 82047.)


File No. I-09-lI2
Page No. 3
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General
By: Dixie
Assistant Division Chief
